meta.title: Security Policy
meta.description: Explains how Aerealith AI protects users, organizations, data, accounts, infrastructure, integrations, automations, APIs, plugins, and marketplace items.

sections.title: 1. Purpose
body: This Security Policy explains how SinLess Games LLC approaches security for Aerealith AI.

Aerealith AI is designed to help users think, create, automate, analyze, monitor, and act across connected systems. Because Aerealith AI may process prompts, files, memory, integrations, automations, plugins, analytics, dashboards, APIs, organization workspaces, infrastructure workflows, and marketplace items, security is a core requirement of the platform.
This policy explains:
bullets: Self-hosted, hybrid, and air-gapped deployment responsibilities

sections.title: 2. Scope
body: This policy applies to Aerealith AI services, including:
This policy applies to:
bullets: SinLess Games LLC employees and authorized operators
Anyone who accesses, manages, integrates with, or operates Aerealith AI

sections.title: 3. Core Security Commitments
body: SinLess Games LLC intends to operate Aerealith AI according to the following security commitments:
orderedItems: Protect user data, organization data, and system integrity.
Use reasonable technical and organizational safeguards.
Apply least-privilege access controls.
Protect data in transit and at rest where practical.
Separate ordinary user content from secrets and credentials.
Use approved secret-management systems for sensitive credentials.
Log and monitor security-relevant activity.
Investigate suspected security incidents.
Provide a responsible way to report vulnerabilities.
Reduce risk from unsafe automations, plugins, integrations, and AI tool use.
Support enterprise and self-hosted security controls where appropriate.
Continuously improve security based on risk, incidents, testing, and operational experience.

sections.title: 4. Relationship to Other Policies
body: This policy works together with other Aerealith AI policies, including:
If there is a conflict between this policy and a written agreement signed by SinLess Games LLC, the written agreement controls only to the extent of that conflict.

sections.title: 5. Shared Responsibility
body: Security is a shared responsibility between SinLess Games LLC, users, organizations, developers, marketplace publishers, and deployment operators.
SinLess Games LLC is responsible for reasonable security controls for Aerealith AI systems operated by SinLess Games LLC.
Users are responsible for securing their accounts, devices, credentials, integrations, automations, API keys, uploaded data, and connected systems.
Organizations are responsible for configuring users, roles, permissions, retention, integrations, audit settings, marketplace controls, automations, and security policies for their own workspaces.
Developers and marketplace publishers are responsible for securing their applications, plugins, APIs, code, dependencies, permissions, data handling, and user-facing disclosures.
Self-hosted, hybrid, and air-gapped deployment operators are responsible for securing their own infrastructure, networks, storage, backups, logs, users, integrations, and deployment configurations.

sections.title: 6. User Security Responsibilities
body: Users must take reasonable steps to protect their Aerealith AI accounts and connected systems.
Users are responsible for:
bullets: Using strong, unique passwords where passwords are used
Protecting passkeys, recovery codes, and authentication devices
Signing out from shared or untrusted devices
Protecting API keys, tokens, passwords, private keys, and secrets
Avoiding secrets in ordinary prompts, files, memory, chats, or support messages
Disconnecting integrations that are no longer needed
Testing generated code and infrastructure changes before production use

sections.title: 7. Organization Security Responsibilities
body: Organizations are responsible for configuring Aerealith AI according to their risk profile.
Organization administrators are responsible for:
bullets: Removing users who no longer need access

sections.title: 1. Purpose
body: This Acceptable Use Policy explains what is and is not allowed when using Aerealith AI, including our websites, applications, APIs, automations, integrations, plugins, marketplace items, documentation, community spaces, and related services.

Aerealith AI is designed to help users think, create, automate, analyze, monitor, and act across connected systems. Because Aerealith AI can interact with data, tools, services, and infrastructure, users must use it responsibly, lawfully, and with proper authorization.

By using Aerealith AI, you agree to follow this policy, our Terms of Use, Privacy Policy, Data Policy, Security Policy, and any other policies that apply to your account, organization, subscription, or deployment.

sections.title: 3. Core Rules

Consistent with this policy and related Aerealith AI policies

sections.title: 1. Purpose
body: This AI Transparency Policy explains how Aerealith AI uses artificial intelligence, automation, memory, integrations, tools, and generated outputs.

Aerealith AI is designed to help users think, create, automate, analyze, monitor, and act across connected systems. Because AI systems can produce inaccurate, incomplete, biased, or unexpected results, this policy explains what users should expect, what Aerealith AI can and cannot do, and when human review is required.
This policy is intended to support transparency, user control, responsible use, and informed decision-making.

sections.title: 2. Scope
body: This policy applies to Aerealith AI, including:

This policy applies to individual users, organizations, developers, administrators, and anyone using Aerealith AI through an account, workspace, API key, integration, shared agent, embedded assistant, or connected service.
bullets: Web, desktop, mobile, browser, terminal, and API experiences
AI chat, assistant, agent, and automation features
Search, retrieval, summarization, and analysis features
Plugins, workflows, dashboards, templates, personalities, and marketplace items
Cloud-hosted, self-hosted, hybrid, and air-gapped deployments, where applicable

sections.title: 3. What Aerealith AI Is
body: Aerealith AI is an artificial intelligence assistant platform operated by SinLess Games LLC.
Aerealith AI may help users:
Aerealith AI is not a human, attorney, doctor, financial advisor, accountant, therapist, emergency responder, government official, or licensed professional.
bullets: Draft, rewrite, summarize, and organize content
Analyze files, messages, code, logs, events, and structured data
Generate ideas, plans, checklists, workflows, and documentation
Search, retrieve, compare, and explain information
Create dashboards, reports, and operational summaries
Assist with development, infrastructure, automation, and technical workflows
Execute approved actions through connected tools, plugins, and integrations
Personalize responses based on user-controlled preferences and memory

sections.title: 4. No Claim of Human Status or Sentience
body: Aerealith AI may use natural language, memory, personas, tone settings, and assistant identities to provide a more helpful experience.
However:
Any references to assistant identity, personality, memory, continuity, or companionship are product features and should not be understood as proof of sentience or human status.
bullets: Aerealith AI does not have personal feelings, consciousness, legal personhood, or independent rights.
Aerealith AI may model empathy behaviorally, but it does not experience emotions.
Aerealith AI may use a name, voice, avatar, or persona, but these are interface and personalization features.
Aerealith AI does not replace the user’s judgment, responsibility, or legal obligations.

sections.title: 5. AI-Generated Output
body: Aerealith AI may generate text, code, summaries, recommendations, classifications, plans, images, structured data, reports, or other outputs.
AI-generated output may be:
Users are responsible for reviewing, validating, testing, and approving AI-generated output before relying on it, publishing it, deploying it, sending it, or using it to make decisions.
bullets: Unsuitable for a specific use case
Based on incomplete or misunderstood context
Affected by user-provided information, retrieved information, tool results, model limitations, or system constraints

sections.title: 6. User Responsibility
body: You are responsible for:
Aerealith AI may assist with decision-making, but it should not be treated as the sole source of truth.
bullets: Reviewing Aerealith AI outputs before use
Verifying facts, numbers, citations, code, configurations, and recommendations
Testing generated code, scripts, automations, infrastructure changes, and workflows
Ensuring your use complies with applicable laws, contracts, policies, and professional obligations
Ensuring you have the right to upload, process, share, or connect data
Confirming that any automated action is authorized, safe, and appropriate
Applying human judgment to high-impact, regulated, sensitive, or irreversible decisions

sections.title: 7. Human Review Requirements
body: Human review is required before using Aerealith AI output for:
Organizations may configure additional review requirements, approval gates, audit controls, or restricted-action policies.
bullets: Legal, medical, financial, tax, insurance, or investment decisions
Employment, hiring, firing, discipline, promotion, or compensation decisions
Housing, education, credit, lending, benefits, eligibility, or essential service decisions
Identity, access, permissions, secrets, or credential management
Public statements, official notices, contracts, regulatory filings, or compliance submissions
Actions that may materially affect another person’s rights, safety, privacy, finances, employment, reputation, or access to services

body: Aerealith AI may help draft, explain, summarize, compare, or organize information related to legal, medical, financial, security, compliance, tax, employment, or business topics.
Aerealith AI does not provide professional advice.
Users should consult a qualified professional before relying on Aerealith AI output for regulated, specialized, or high-impact matters.

body: Aerealith AI may use information from:

When citations, links, source references, or provenance are provided, they are intended to help users verify the answer. Citations do not guarantee that the output is complete, correct, current, or suitable for a particular use.

body: Aerealith AI may not always know the latest information.

News, public events, elections, weather, sports, and market data

body: Aerealith AI may support memory and personalization features.

Depending on user and organization settings, Aerealith AI may remember information such as:

body: Aerealith AI may use tools, APIs, plugins, integrations, and automations to perform actions or retrieve information.

body: Sensitive data may include:
Users should avoid entering secrets, credentials, private keys, passwords, or access tokens into prompts unless a feature is specifically designed and approved to handle that information securely.
Secrets should be stored in approved secret-management systems, not in ordinary chat memory or general user content.
bullets: Credentials, passwords, tokens, private keys, and secrets

meta.title: Responsible AI Policy
meta.description: Explains Aerealith AI principles, safeguards, governance, human oversight, safety testing, risk management, and responsible use of artificial intelligence.

sections.title: 1. Purpose
body: This Responsible AI Policy explains how SinLess Games LLC approaches the responsible design, development, deployment, monitoring, and use of artificial intelligence in Aerealith AI.

Aerealith AI is designed to help users think, create, automate, analyze, monitor, and act across connected systems. Because AI can affect decisions, workflows, content, data, infrastructure, organizations, and people, Aerealith AI must be built and operated with clear safeguards, user control, accountability, transparency, and human oversight.
This policy explains:

sections.title: 2. Scope
body: This policy applies to Aerealith AI services, including:
This policy applies to:
bullets: Generated images, audio, video, or other media, where supported
SinLess Games LLC employees, contractors, and authorized operators
Anyone who builds, configures, deploys, uses, or manages Aerealith AI AI-powered features

sections.title: 3. Core Responsible AI Principles
body: Aerealith AI should be developed and operated according to these principles:
orderedItems: User agency: Users should remain in control of important decisions, connected tools, automations, memory, integrations, and data.
Transparency: Users should understand when they are interacting with AI, what AI may do, what its limits are, and when verification is required.
Human oversight: High-impact, sensitive, regulated, destructive, or irreversible actions should require appropriate human review.
Safety: Aerealith AI should reduce the risk of harm to users, organizations, systems, data, and the public.
Privacy and data protection: Aerealith AI should process data only for legitimate service business logic and approved research purposes.
Security: AI features should be designed to resist abuse, unauthorized access, prompt injection, unsafe tool use, and data leakage.
Fairness and bias awareness: Aerealith AI should be evaluated for harmful bias and should not be used as the sole basis for high-impact decisions about people.

Responsible extensibility: Plugins, marketplace items, developer tools, and automations should follow clear safety, permission, and review requirements.

Add-ons may renew with the main subscription or on their own billing schedule.

meta.title: Copyright Takedown Policy
meta.description: Explains how Aerealith AI handles copyright complaints, DMCA notices, counter-notices, repeat infringers, and copyright-related takedown requests.

sections.title: 1. Purpose
body: This Copyright Takedown Policy explains how SinLess Games LLC handles copyright complaints, takedown requests, counter-notices, repeat infringers, and copyright-related disputes involving Aerealith AI.

Aerealith AI may allow users, organizations, developers, marketplace publishers, and third-party integrations to upload, generate, store, publish, share, transform, summarize, distribute, or otherwise process content. Because that content may include copyrighted material, this policy describes how copyright owners may report alleged infringement and how affected users may respond.
This policy is intended to support lawful copyright enforcement, fair dispute handling, and responsible platform operation.

sections.title: 2. Scope
body: This policy applies to Aerealith AI services, including:

This policy applies to users, account owners, organization administrators, developers, marketplace publishers, copyright owners, authorized agents, and anyone who submits or receives a copyright-related notice through Aerealith AI.
bullets: Cloud-hosted, self-hosted, hybrid, and air-gapped deployments, where applicable

sections.title: 3. Relationship to Other Policies
body: This policy works together with other Aerealith AI policies, including:
If there is a conflict between this policy and a written agreement signed by SinLess Games LLC, the written agreement controls only to the extent of that conflict.

sections.title: 4. No Legal Advice
body: This policy is provided for informational and operational purposes.
It is not legal advice.

Copyright law can be complex, and rights may vary by country, jurisdiction, content type, license, contract, and use case. If you are unsure about your rights or obligations, consult a qualified attorney.

sections.title: 5. Copyright Ownership and User Responsibility
body: Users are responsible for ensuring that they have the rights, licenses, permissions, exceptions, or legal basis needed to upload, generate, store, process, publish, distribute, share, modify, transform, or use content through Aerealith AI.
You may not use Aerealith AI to infringe another person’s copyright or other intellectual property rights.
You are responsible for content and activity associated with your account, organization, workspace, API keys, automations, plugins, integrations, marketplace items, and users.

sections.title: 6. Prohibited Copyright Activity
body: You may not use Aerealith AI to:
bullets: Upload, publish, share, or distribute copyrighted material without authorization
Copy, reproduce, or distribute protected works without permission or legal basis
Remove, obscure, or alter copyright notices, license terms, attribution, or provenance information
Circumvent digital rights management, license checks, paywalls, or technical protection measures
Create or distribute tools intended to enable copyright infringement
Publish marketplace items containing content you do not have rights to use
Misrepresent ownership or licensing of content
Use Aerealith AI to mass-copy, scrape, or redistribute copyrighted material in violation of law, contract, or platform rules
Repeatedly upload or publish infringing content after notice

sections.title: 7. DMCA Designated Agent
body: SinLess Games LLC should designate an agent to receive notices of claimed copyright infringement under the Digital Millennium Copyright Act.
Before publishing this policy, SinLess Games LLC should confirm that the designated agent information has been registered with the U.S. Copyright Office and is accurate.
Copyright notices should be sent to the designated agent listed below.
Before publishing this policy, confirm that the email alias, mailing address, and phone number are valid, monitored, and match the information registered with the U.S. Copyright Office.

sections.title: 8. Copyright Takedown Notice Requirements
body: If you believe content available through Aerealith AI infringes your copyright, you or your authorized agent may submit a written takedown notice to our designated agent.
Your notice should include:
Incomplete notices may delay processing.
orderedItems: A physical or electronic signature of the copyright owner or a person authorized to act on behalf of the copyright owner.
Identification of the copyrighted work claimed to have been infringed.
If multiple copyrighted works are covered by one notice, a representative list of those works.
Identification of the allegedly infringing material or activity.
Information reasonably sufficient to allow us to locate the allegedly infringing material or activity.
Your contact information, including name, mailing address, telephone number, and email address.
A statement that you have a good-faith belief that use of the material in the manner complained of is not authorized by the copyright owner, the copyright owner’s agent, or law.
A statement that the information in the notice is accurate.
A statement, under penalty of perjury, that you are the copyright owner or are authorized to act on behalf of the copyright owner.

sections.title: 9. Helpful Information to Include
body: To help us process a copyright complaint, include as much detail as possible.
Helpful information may include:
Do not include unnecessary sensitive information.
bullets: The URL or exact location of the allegedly infringing content
The account, workspace, marketplace item, plugin, file, automation, or integration involved
A description of the original copyrighted work
Links to authorized copies of the copyrighted work
Explanation of how the content allegedly infringes
Proof that you are authorized to act for the copyright owner, if submitting as an agent

sections.title: 10. Where to Send Takedown Notices
body: Send copyright takedown notices to copyright@sinlessgames.com.
You may also send notices by mail to the designated agent address listed in this policy.
Before publishing this policy, confirm whether mail, email, or both will be accepted for formal notices.

sections.title: 11. Processing Takedown Notices
body: When we receive a copyright takedown notice, we may:
bullets: Remove or disable access to the allegedly infringing material
Notify the affected user, account owner, workspace administrator, or marketplace publisher
Preserve records related to the complaint
Forward the notice to the affected user or organization
Disable marketplace items, plugins, workflows, templates, or integrations
Restrict accounts, workspaces, API keys, automations, or publishing features
Decline to act if the notice is incomplete, invalid, abusive, fraudulent, or unrelated to copyright
We may act quickly when required or appropriate, but submission of a notice does not guarantee removal.

sections.title: 12. User Notice After Removal

The notice may include:
We may withhold some information if required by law, policy, safety, privacy, security, or abuse-prevention needs.
bullets: A copy or summary of the takedown notice

Any account, workspace, marketplace, or feature restrictions applied

sections.title: 13. Counter-Notice
body: If your content was removed or disabled because of a copyright takedown notice and you believe the removal was mistaken or the material was misidentified, you may submit a written counter-notice.
Your counter-notice should include:
Counter-notices should be sent to copyright@sinlessgames.com.
orderedItems: Your physical or electronic signature.
Identification of the material that was removed or disabled.
The location where the material appeared before it was removed or disabled.
A statement, under penalty of perjury, that you have a good-faith belief that the material was removed or disabled because of mistake or misidentification.
Your name, mailing address, telephone number, and email address.
A statement that you consent to the jurisdiction of the federal district court for the judicial district where your address is located.
If your address is outside the United States, a statement that you consent to the jurisdiction of any judicial district where SinLess Games LLC may be found.
A statement that you will accept service of process from the person who submitted the original takedown notice or that person’s agent.

sections.title: 14. Counter-Notice Processing
body: When we receive a valid counter-notice, we may forward it to the party that submitted the original takedown notice.

We may restore the removed or disabled material after the legally required waiting period unless the original complaining party notifies us that they have filed a court action seeking to restrain the user from engaging in the allegedly infringing activity.
We may decline to restore content if:
bullets: The counter-notice appears fraudulent or abusive
The content violates another Aerealith AI policy
The content creates security, privacy, safety, or legal risk
The user’s account has been suspended or terminated for other reasons
A court order, legal obligation, or written agreement prevents restoration

sections.title: 15. Repeat Infringer Policy
body: SinLess Games LLC may terminate or restrict accounts, users, organizations, workspaces, marketplace publishers, plugins, integrations, or developer access for repeat copyright infringement.
Repeat infringement may include:
Enforcement may include:
The exact action may depend on the severity, frequency, context, validity of notices, user response, and legal obligations.
bullets: Repeated uploads of the same or similar infringing material
Repeated publication of infringing marketplace items
Repeated attempts to evade takedown enforcement
Repeated use of alternate accounts to publish infringing content
Repeated copyright abuse through APIs, automations, plugins, or integrations
Repeated failure to address copyright complaints after notice

sections.title: 16. Standard Technical Measures
body: SinLess Games LLC respects standard technical measures used by copyright owners to identify or protect copyrighted works when such measures meet applicable legal requirements.

Users may not interfere with or circumvent standard technical measures, rights-management systems, license checks, provenance systems, watermarking systems, content protection mechanisms, or other lawful copyright-protection controls.

sections.title: 17. False, Fraudulent, or Abusive Notices
body: Do not submit false, misleading, fraudulent, abusive, or bad-faith takedown notices or counter-notices.
Misrepresenting that content or activity is infringing, or that content was removed by mistake or misidentification, may result in legal liability.
We may reject, limit, or take action against notices that appear to be:

bullets: Unsupported by copyright ownership or authorization

meta.title: Subprocessor and Vendor List
meta.description: Lists subprocessors, vendors, service providers, infrastructure providers, AI providers, payment processors, analytics providers, and other third parties used or planned for Aerealith AI.

sections.title: 1. Purpose
body: This Subprocessor and Vendor List identifies third parties that SinLess Games LLC may use to provide, secure, operate, support, monitor, bill, analyze, improve, and maintain Aerealith AI.
Aerealith AI is operated by SinLess Games LLC.
This list is intended to provide transparency about vendors that may process, store, transmit, access, or support data related to Aerealith AI.
This document should be read together with:

sections.title: 2. Core Commitments
body: SinLess Games LLC makes the following commitments for Aerealith AI:
orderedItems: Users own their data.
Aerealith AI does not sell user data.
Aerealith AI does not use user data outside legitimate service business logic and approved research purposes.
Vendors and subprocessors should only process data for authorized Aerealith AI purposes.
Subprocessors should be reviewed before use where practical.
Sensitive data should receive stronger protection.
Enterprise customers should receive appropriate notice of material subprocessor changes where required by contract.
Self-hosted and air-gapped deployments may use different vendors or no external vendors depending on customer configuration.

sections.title: 3. Scope
body: This list applies to Aerealith AI services, including:
Some vendors may apply only to specific plans, regions, features, integrations, deployment types, or customer configurations.

sections.title: 4. Definitions
body: For this list:

sections.title: 4.1 Subprocessor
body: A subprocessor is a third party that may process personal data, Customer Data, Account Data, Service Data, or other protected data on behalf of SinLess Games LLC to provide Aerealith AI.
Examples may include hosting providers, database providers, AI model providers, payment processors, support tools, observability tools, email providers, and analytics providers.

sections.title: 4.2 Vendor
body: A vendor is a third party that provides products or services to SinLess Games LLC.
Some vendors are subprocessors. Others may not process user data directly.

sections.title: 4.3 Service Provider
body: A service provider is a third party that performs a business function for SinLess Games LLC or Aerealith AI.

sections.title: 4.4 Customer-Controlled Vendor
body: A customer-controlled vendor is a vendor selected, configured, connected, or operated by the user or organization.

Examples include customer GitHub accounts, customer Google Drive accounts, customer Discord servers, customer cloud providers, customer identity providers, customer observability stacks, and customer self-hosted infrastructure.

sections.title: 5. Vendor Categories
body: Aerealith AI vendors may fall into the following categories:

sections.title: 6. Current and Planned Vendor List
body: The list below includes current, planned, or expected vendors for Aerealith AI based on the intended architecture.
Before publishing this document, confirm each vendor’s actual use, data access, region, contract status, and whether the vendor is active, planned, optional, or deprecated.
bullets: Vercel — Hosting / frontend deployment — Hosts Next.js frontend, edge functions, deployments, previews, and web application infrastructure. Data processed may include account metadata, request metadata, IP addresses, logs, frontend app data, and limited Customer Data depending on request flow. Role: Subprocessor. Status: Planned / Confirm.

Cloudflare — DNS / edge / security / CDN — DNS, CDN, DDoS protection, WAF, edge routing, Workers, tunnels, caching, TLS, and security controls. Data processed may include IP addresses, request metadata, headers, logs, cached assets, security events, and limited Customer Data depending on routing. Role: Subprocessor. Status: Planned / Confirm.

Supabase — Database / authentication / storage — Postgres database, pgvector, authentication support, and object storage where configured. Data processed may include Account Data, Customer Data, Service Data, memory, embeddings, files, workspace data, and organization data. Role: Subprocessor. Status: Planned / Confirm.

Redis provider / managed Redis — Cache / queues / sessions — Caching, rate limits, queues, session support, short-term memory, and automation state. Data processed may include session data, cache data, queue payloads, metadata, and temporary Customer Data depending on feature. Role: Subprocessor. Status: Planned / Confirm Provider.

OpenAI — AI model provider — AI inference, model responses, embeddings, analysis, generation, reasoning, and AI-powered features where enabled. Data processed may include prompts, responses, context, retrieved content, metadata, files, or file excerpts where used. Role: Subprocessor. Status: Planned / Confirm.

Other AI providers — AI model provider — Optional model routing, fallback inference, specialized model use, and local or cloud model choices. Data processed may include prompts, responses, context, retrieved content, metadata, files, or file excerpts where used. Role: Subprocessor. Status: Optional / TBD.

Stripe — Payments / billing — Subscription billing, checkout, invoices, payment method handling, fraud prevention, taxes, usage billing, and marketplace payments where enabled. Data processed may include Billing Data, payment metadata, transaction records, invoice data, tax data, fraud signals, and customer identifiers. Role: Subprocessor. Status: Planned / Confirm.

PayPal — Payments / billing — Payment processing, subscriptions, checkout, customer payments, and refunds where enabled. Data processed may include Billing Data, payment metadata, transaction records, customer identifiers, and dispute records. Role: Subprocessor. Status: Planned / Confirm.

Hypertune — Feature flags / experimentation — Feature flags, entitlements, plan gating, controlled rollout, and experimentation. Data processed may include user IDs, tenant IDs, feature flag metadata, entitlement data, plan data, and usage context. Role: Subprocessor. Status: Planned / Confirm.

Google Analytics — Analytics / marketing — Website analytics, documentation usage, public marketing page measurement, and conversion analytics where enabled. Data processed may include cookie identifiers, device metadata, page views, approximate location, referral data, and usage events. Role: Subprocessor. Status: Planned / Confirm.

Grafana Cloud — Observability / monitoring — Metrics, dashboards, logs, traces, profiles, frontend telemetry, and service monitoring. Data processed may include logs, metrics, traces, errors, metadata, service health data, and limited request data depending on configuration. Role: Subprocessor. Status: Planned / Confirm.

Grafana Faro — Frontend observability — Browser telemetry, frontend errors, performance monitoring, and user experience monitoring. Data processed may include browser metadata, errors, page metadata, performance data, and limited user or session identifiers. Role: Subprocessor. Status: Planned / Confirm.

Contentful — CMS / content management — Marketing content, documentation content, public site content, and content workflows. Data processed may include content metadata, editor account data, public content, and limited user interaction metadata where configured. Role: Subprocessor. Status: Planned / Confirm.

GitHub — Source control / CI/CD / developer workflows — Source control, issue tracking, pull requests, GitHub Actions, self-hosted runner coordination, and release workflows. Data processed may include repository data, developer account data, CI/CD metadata, issue or PR data, and workflow logs. Role: Vendor / Subprocessor depending on use. Status: Planned / Confirm.

Google Workspace / Gmail / Calendar / Drive APIs — User-authorized integrations — User-authorized email, calendar, contacts, Drive, Docs, Sheets, and Slides integrations. Data processed may include data authorized by user or organization, including emails, files, calendar data, contacts, and metadata. Role: Customer-Controlled Integration / Subprocessor depending on feature. Status: Optional.

Discord — User-authorized integration / community — Community spaces, notifications, bots, ChatOps, automations, and support or community operations where enabled. Data processed may include Discord IDs, messages, server metadata, channel metadata, and bot interaction data. Role: Customer-Controlled Integration / Vendor. Status: Optional / Planned.

Twitch — User-authorized integration — Creator and streamer integrations, automation triggers, chat, or stream-related workflows where enabled. Data processed may include Twitch account IDs, stream metadata, chat metadata, and automation data. Role: Customer-Controlled Integration / Vendor. Status: Optional / Planned.

Cloudflare R2 — Object storage — Storage for backups, files, logs, analytics exports, observability archives, or customer assets where configured. Data processed may include files, backups, logs, object metadata, and Customer Data depending on storage use. Role: Subprocessor. Status: Optional / Planned.

Email service provider — Email delivery — Transactional email, account notices, billing notices, incident notices, security notices, and support communications. Data processed may include email address, name, message metadata, email content, and delivery events. Role: Subprocessor. Status: TBD.

Customer support provider — Support / helpdesk — Support tickets, customer communications, troubleshooting, and support history. Data processed may include contact details, support messages, screenshots, logs provided by user, and account metadata. Role: Subprocessor. Status: TBD.

Status page provider — Incident communication — Public or customer status page, incident updates, maintenance notices, and subscriber alerts. Data processed may include subscriber email, incident communication data, and service status data. Role: Subprocessor. Status: TBD.

Tax calculation provider — Tax / billing — Sales tax, VAT, GST, digital tax calculation, tax reporting, and tax exemption handling. Data processed may include billing address, tax IDs, invoice records, and transaction metadata. Role: Subprocessor. Status: TBD.

Accounting provider — Accounting / finance — Bookkeeping, revenue tracking, invoices, financial reporting, and tax preparation support. Data processed may include billing records, customer billing metadata, invoice records, and payment records. Role: Vendor / Subprocessor depending on use. Status: TBD.

Legal and compliance providers — Legal / compliance — Legal review, compliance support, data processing agreements, privacy requests, and contract management. Data processed may include contract data, legal notices, customer contact data, and incident records where needed. Role: Vendor / Subprocessor depending on use. Status: TBD.

Security scanning providers — Security / vulnerability management — Dependency scanning, code scanning, container scanning, secret scanning, and vulnerability review. Data processed may include source code, dependency metadata, security findings, and CI/CD metadata. Role: Vendor / Subprocessor depending on use. Status: TBD.

Error monitoring provider — Error reporting — Application error tracking, exceptions, and performance diagnostics. Data processed may include error logs, stack traces, request metadata, device metadata, and limited user identifiers. Role: Subprocessor. Status: TBD.

Object storage provider — File storage / backups — File uploads, generated artifacts, exports, backups, marketplace assets, and support attachments. Data processed may include files, exports, backups, metadata, and Customer Data depending on use. Role: Subprocessor. Status: TBD.

Identity provider integrations — Authentication / SSO — OAuth, SAML, OIDC, SSO, and enterprise identity or directory sync where enabled. Data processed may include login identifiers, email addresses, names, group membership, and role metadata. Role: Customer-Controlled Integration / Subprocessor depending on configuration. Status: Optional.

Customer cloud providers — Self-hosted / hybrid / air-gapped — Customer-operated infrastructure for private deployments. Data processed is controlled by customer deployment. Role: Customer-Controlled Vendor. Status: Optional.

Customer observability systems — Self-hosted / enterprise monitoring — Customer-managed logs, metrics, traces, dashboards, SIEM, and alerting. Data processed may include logs, metrics, traces, audit records, and service telemetry. Role: Customer-Controlled Vendor. Status: Optional.

Customer secret managers — Secrets / key management — Vault, KMS, cloud secret managers, customer-managed encryption, and secret references. Data processed may include secrets, keys, tokens, credentials, and secret metadata. Role: Customer-Controlled Vendor. Status: Optional.

meta.title: Underage Policy
meta.description: Explains how Aerealith AI handles minors, age-restricted content, parental controls, guardian consent, child safety, age assurance, and underage account protections.

sections.title: 1. Purpose
body: This Underage Policy explains how SinLess Games LLC handles minors, age-restricted content, parental controls, guardian consent, child safety, and underage account protections for Aerealith AI.

Aerealith AI is designed to help users think, create, automate, analyze, monitor, and act across connected systems. Because some Aerealith AI features may involve AI conversations, memory, files, integrations, automations, marketplace items, user-generated content, analytics, and connected services, minors require additional protections.
SinLess Games LLC will use lawful and verifiable means to help protect age-restricted content and will provide parental or guardian controls for minors where supported and legally required.

sections.title: 2. Scope
body: This policy applies to Aerealith AI services, including:
This policy applies to:
bullets: Anyone who accesses or uses Aerealith AI through an account, workspace, integration, automation, shared agent, plugin, API key, or connected service

sections.title: 3. Relationship to Other Policies
body: This policy works together with other Aerealith AI policies, including:
If there is a conflict between this policy and a written agreement signed by SinLess Games LLC, the written agreement controls only to the extent of that conflict.

sections.title: 4. Definitions
body: For this policy:
bullets: “Minor” means a person who has not reached the age of majority in their jurisdiction.
“Child” means a minor under the age threshold where parental consent or special child-privacy protections are required by applicable law.
“Teen” means a minor who is old enough to use certain online services with restrictions but has not reached the age of majority.
“Parent” or “guardian” means a person with legal authority to act for a minor.

“Age-restricted content” means content, features, services, marketplace items, integrations, automations, or materials that are not appropriate for minors or may legally require age verification, parental controls, restricted access, or additional safeguards.
“Age assurance” means a method used to estimate, verify, or confirm a user’s age or age range.

“Parental controls” means account, workspace, device, organization, or product settings that allow a parent, guardian, school, or authorized administrator to manage a minor’s access, privacy, safety, spending, communication, content, memory, integrations, and automations.

sections.title: 5. Core Commitments
body: SinLess Games LLC intends to follow these underage safety commitments for Aerealith AI:
orderedItems: Protect minors from inappropriate and age-restricted content.
Use lawful and verifiable age-assurance methods where required or appropriate.
Provide parental or guardian controls for minors where supported and legally required.
Avoid collecting unnecessary data from minors.
Avoid selling user data, including minors’ data.
Avoid using minors’ private data outside legitimate service business logic and approved research purposes.
Restrict high-risk AI features for minors.
Restrict mature, sexual, exploitative, violent, harmful, or regulated content for minors.
Restrict unsafe automations, public sharing, marketplace access, and integrations for minors where appropriate.
Give parents, guardians, and authorized administrators tools to manage minor accounts where supported.
Treat child safety reports with high priority.
Remove or restrict underage access when required by law, policy, or safety needs.

sections.title: 6. Minimum Age
body: Aerealith AI may require users to meet a minimum age before creating an account or using certain features.
Before publishing this policy, SinLess Games LLC should confirm the official minimum age for Aerealith AI.
Recommended default:
Local law may require different age thresholds.
bullets: Users under 13 may not create or use personal Aerealith AI accounts unless a legally valid parent, guardian, school, or organization arrangement is in place.
Users ages 13 to 17 may use Aerealith AI only with age-appropriate restrictions and any required parent, guardian, school, or organization consent.

Users must be at least 18, or the age of majority in their jurisdiction, to access age-restricted content, mature content, unrestricted marketplace purchases, payment features, or other adult-only features.

sections.title: 7. Age-Restricted Content
body: Aerealith AI may restrict access to content or features that are not appropriate for minors.
Age-restricted content or features may include:
SinLess Games LLC may block, hide, limit, label, age-gate, or remove age-restricted content.
bullets: Mature companionship or romantic persona modes
Self-harm content that is not safety-oriented
Mature plugins, personalities, workflows, templates, or prompt packs
High-risk financial, legal, medical, insurance, employment, or housing decision workflows
Public publishing features that expose minors to unwanted contact
Direct messaging or community features that are not age-appropriate
Any other feature restricted by law, platform policy, payment processor rules, or SinLess Games LLC policy

sections.title: 8. Age Assurance
body: SinLess Games LLC may use lawful and verifiable age-assurance methods to protect minors and restrict age-restricted content.
Age-assurance methods may include:
SinLess Games LLC should use the least intrusive method reasonably suitable for the risk level.
Age-assurance data should be minimized, protected, retained only as needed, and deleted or de-identified where appropriate.
bullets: Payment-method verification, where lawful and appropriate
Government ID verification, where legally required and proportionate
Device, app-store, or platform age settings, where available
Other lawful methods appropriate to the feature and jurisdiction

sections.title: 9. Age Verification Data
body: Age verification may involve sensitive personal information.
SinLess Games LLC should limit collection and retention of age verification data.

body: Aerealith AI may provide parental or guardian controls where supported.

bullets: “User Content” means content submitted, uploaded, imported, connected, generated, transformed, published, distributed, or otherwise processed by a user through Aerealith AI.

“Input” means content a user provides to Aerealith AI, including prompts, files, messages, code, data, instructions, images, audio, video, documents, logs, links, integration data, and other materials.

“Output” means content generated, edited, transformed, summarized, analyzed, classified, formatted, or otherwise produced by Aerealith AI in response to user input, tool results, files, integrations, automations, or other context.

“AI-Assisted Content” means content created with material help from Aerealith AI, including generated text, code, images, reports, workflows, dashboards, scripts, summaries, designs, or other materials.

“Public Content” means User Content made visible outside a private account or workspace, including shared links, public dashboards, marketplace listings, plugins, templates, documentation, community posts, published reports, or other externally visible materials.
“Attribution” means a reasonable notice that Aerealith AI was used to generate, assist, transform, or support the content.
